description_long: This house is a multi-level, stilt-supported residential structure composed of several interconnected boxy units, each with its own flat roof and outdoor terrace. The exterior features a mix of neutral tones, including gray, beige, and brown, with some units displaying brickwork and others smooth concrete or siding. Numerous balconies and rooftop patios are furnished with tables, chairs, and umbrellas, suggesting communal and private outdoor spaces. The house is elevated on tall columns, creating open space beneath and a network of walkways connecting the various sections. Windows are arranged irregularly, with some large panes and others smaller, contributing to an eclectic, modern urban aesthetic. The overall design is modular and functional, with visible staircases, railings, and a few distinctive architectural elements such as a yellow chimney and rooftop structures.
description: A large, multi-story modular house on stilts with interconnected terraces, flat roofs, and a mix of brick, concrete, and siding in neutral colors. Features include rooftop patios, irregular window placement, and elevated walkways.
description_view_0: Front view shows the main entryways, multiple terraces, and the prominent yellow chimney, with a clear view of the stilt supports and walkways.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the rear terraces, rooftop patios with umbrellas, and the staggered arrangement of the house units.
description_view_2: Side view emphasizes the elevated structure, flat roofs, and the open space beneath the house, with visible outdoor furniture on the patios.
description_view_3: Opposite side view reveals more of the modular units, additional balconies, and the varied window arrangements, as well as the stilt supports.
attribute_entryway_design: Multiple entryways with steps, simple doors, and minimal overhangs; some units have small porches or terraces with railings.
